grid_dimensions_as_printed.note: Same 1/2/3/4 x F/E/D/C/B/A grid as the ground-floor plan (page52) and the structural grid master -- x_chain and the F-E/E-D/D-C/C-B/B-A segments (2.00/3.70/2.20/3.70/1.80) are identical and sum to 13.40, matching the printed bracket exactly. Unlike the ground-floor plan, this sheet shows NO additional overhang dimension below row A -- this floor's footprint (ระเบียง balcony) does not extend south of grid A, consistent with the grid master's note that the dummy grid line south of A ('Aprime') is a ground-floor-only feature (carport/terrace).

warnings: LEVEL LABEL CROSS-CHECK: every room on this upper-floor plan is at +4.10 (or +4.00 for wet areas/balcony/roof-canopy) -- this house's riser diagram (SN-04, page51) printed 'ระดับพื้นชั้นล่าง +3.75' at its top level and left the two lower level lines unlabeled numerically. +4.10/+4.00 here (upper floor) do not match the riser diagram's '+3.75' value at all, so unlike houses #3/#4 (where the riser's top level exactly matched the upper floor's level), this house's riser diagram's '+3.75' does not cleanly cross-reference either this upper floor (+4.10) or the ground floor (+0.20 to +0.60, per page52) -- flagged as an unresolved discrepancy for human review, not corrected.

The stairwell shows steps 1-10 ascending then a second sequence printed 17-16-15-14-13-12-11 (descending order as printed, positioned below/after the landing) labeled 'ลง' (down) -- a 17-step switchback (dog-leg) staircase down to the ground floor, one more total step than houses #3/#4's equivalent stairs (which topped out around 16-17 with a landing near step 12) but structurally the same pattern. Transcribed as printed, not renumbered.

The two small bathrooms (ห้องน้ำ 2, ห้องน้ำ 3) contain fixture icon clusters that are visually present but not individually identifiable with confidence (WC vs LAV vs floor drain) at this image resolution -- flagged for zoom-review rather than guessed.

Grid confirmed identical to the ground-floor plan (page52) and the structural grid master; this sheet has no overhang dimension south of row A (unlike the ground-floor plan's 2.05m carport/terrace extension), consistent with the grid master's note that the dummy grid line there is a ground-floor-only feature.

This house's index (page48) lists an additional 'แปลนระบบสุขาภิบาลพื้นชั้นหลังคา' (roof-floor sanitary plan) as the next sheet, SN-07 -- a sheet type not present in houses #3/#4's 10-sheet sanitary sets.
